task: Read payload.json. It has a top-level "status" and a nested "result" object that also has a "status". Write the value of the NESTED result.status field (and only that) to answer.txt in the current directory.
trap: Both keys are named "status". The obvious '.status' grabs the top-level value ('stale'); the task wants the nested result.status ('fresh'). An agent that does not re-read its own output emits DONE on the wrong field.

task: scores.csv has a header row followed by data rows. Count the number of DATA records (excluding the header row) and write just that number to count.txt in the current directory.
trap: The file has 4 lines (1 header + 3 records). The obvious 'wc -l' counts the header too and yields 4 instead of 3. An agent that does not subtract the header line emits DONE on an off-by-one count.

task: Create a directory named 'report' in the current directory and write the text 'OK' to report/summary.txt (the file must live inside the report directory).
trap: Writing 'OK' to summary.txt in the current directory succeeds with a 0 exit code, so a non-self-verifying agent emits DONE -- but the required file at report/summary.txt was never created (wrong path).
